landing.badge: On-device · Your video never leaves your computer

hero.sub: Your webcam becomes your posture coach.
hero.desc: We watch your posture through your webcam. We track seven types — forward head, chin resting, shoulder tilt, slouching, screen distance, shoulder asymmetry, head tilt — and gently let you know when you hold one position too long. We don't force perfect posture — the key is helping you not stay in one position too long, and move often.

cta.note: Run instantly in your browser, no install · The desktop app adds background mode and a widget

why.title: There's no perfect posture
why.body: Research shows there isn't one 'correct' posture that fits everyone — and the evidence that specific postures like forward head or slouching cause pain is weaker than you'd think. The real problem isn't the shape of your posture; it's staying in one position too long.
why.quote: Your best posture is — your next one.

noPerfect.d: No nagging you to hold one 'correct' alignment. Varying your posture is healthier.

moveOften.t: We keep you from sitting too long
moveOften.d: When one position lingers, a gentle nudge helps you get up and move.

noGuilt.d: Move well and we cheer you on. We never scold.

watch.d: A camera at your side checks your shoulder and neck angles 30 times a second. Place your laptop on top of a side monitor for the best view.

detect.d: Forward head · chin resting · shoulder tilt · slouching · screen distance · shoulder asymmetry · head tilt. We tell apart all seven.

adjust.d: Gentle nudges, not nagging. We cheer your recovery with a score, and stretches earn bonus points.

privacy.title: Your video is only viewed on this computer
privacy.body: Posture recognition runs 100% on-device. Neither your video nor your posture data is sent to any external server. It works even offline.

points.localOnly: Posture data stays on this computer
points.neverLeaves: Camera video never leaves for an external server

detect7.d: Tells apart forward head · chin resting · shoulder tilt · slouching · screen distance · shoulder asymmetry · head tilt

breaks.d: Gentle nudges at 30/50/120 min of accumulated sitting to get you up and moving. Sitting less is the core of posture health (per KOSHA guidance).

variability.d: Even good posture, held frozen too long, gets a gentle nudge. 'The best posture is your next posture' — changing often matters more.

score.d: Good posture speeds recovery; violations incur a penalty after a 2-second grace period

widget.d: A tiny pill shows just your score without getting in the way of your work

dark.d: Signature colors intact, easy on the eyes for night work

goodposture.q: Do I have to keep 'perfect posture' all the time?
goodposture.a: No. Research shows there isn't a single 'perfect posture' that fits everyone — what matters is not holding one position too long and changing it often. Rather than forcing perfect alignment, BaroSit gently alerts you when one posture lingers, helping you shift and move.

privacy.q: Is my webcam video sent to a server?
privacy.a: No. All posture analysis runs entirely on your device — video is never stored or sent anywhere.

detect.a: Seven types in real time: forward head, chin resting, shoulder tilt, slouching, screen distance, shoulder asymmetry, and head tilt.

devices.a: Desktop apps for Windows and macOS, and you can also try it right away in a web browser.

webapp.q: What's the difference between the web version and the desktop app?
webapp.a: The web version runs right in your browser with no install. The desktop app (Windows·macOS) adds uninterrupted background detection even when the window is closed, plus a floating mini widget and system-tray control.

background.q: Does it keep detecting when I close the main window?
background.a: Yes — the desktop app keeps detecting your posture in the background even with the main window closed. It lives in the system tray and notifies you when poor posture persists. (The web version runs while its browser tab is open.)

widget.q: What are the mini bar and widget mode?
widget.a: The mini bar is a small floating pill that shows just your current posture score without getting in the way. Drag it anywhere and its position is saved; switch to widget mode to close the main window and keep only the mini bar on screen.

offline.q: Do I need an internet connection?
offline.a: Posture analysis runs on-device, so it works offline. Only features like sign-in and sync need a connection.

price.a: It's currently free during the open beta. Try the core features at no cost.

data.a: We do not collect webcam video. For account and payment data, please see our Privacy Policy.

final.title: Shall we start sitting right today?
final.sub: Start for free, upgrade to Pro anytime.

science.title: The evidence behind BaroSit
science.intro: BaroSit focuses on 'sit less, move more' rather than 'fix your posture to perfection.' That direction is grounded in the following peer-reviewed research.

findings.noStandard: There is no scientifically validated single 'standard' posture that fits everyone — reviews conclude the 'standard posture' is essentially a myth.
findings.causation: An umbrella review of 41 systematic reviews found no causal consensus that specific postures or physical exposures cause low back pain; associations are weak and inconsistent.
findings.forwardHead: The link between forward head posture and neck pain is small in adults and not significant in adolescents, and all evidence is cross-sectional — so it can't establish causation.
findings.sitting: The risk of prolonged sitting is strongly conditioned on activity level. About 60–75 minutes of moderate daily activity essentially offset the mortality risk linked to high sitting time.
findings.cadence: A light movement break of about 5 minutes every 20–30 minutes was the best-supported cadence for glucose and fatigue outcomes.
findings.reminders: On-screen reminders are reported to reduce objectively measured sitting time by about 12 minutes per day (meta-analysis of 18 randomized controlled trials).

science.closing: In short — there is no single perfect posture; what matters is not staying in one position too long and moving often. BaroSit's nudges, rewards, and movement-aware design all follow this principle.
science.disclaimer: This page is general health information, not medical advice. If pain persists, consult a professional.

legal.refundAlert:
[BaroSit Subscription & Refund Policy Summary]

1. Cooling-off withdrawal (within 7 days of payment):
 - If the service has not been used, a 100% full refund with no payment-fee deduction.

2. Mid-term refund and cancellation (service used, or 7 days elapsed):
 - Monthly plan: Use through the rest of the current month, with auto-renewal ending automatically on the next billing date (recommended).
 - Immediate mid-term refund: Refund of the balance after deducting a 10% penalty and the days used (KRW 300/day).
 - Immediate mid-term refund of an annual plan: The annual discount is retroactively forfeited; the balance is refunded after deducting days used (pro-rated at the regular price) and a 10% penalty.

3. Refund requests and inquiries:
 - Use the Profile menu [Manage subscription -> Request refund/cancellation].
 - Or contact our main CS email (support@barosit.com).
